title: Bilateral Joint Pain - Rheumatoid Arthritis
chief_complaint: Bilateral hand and wrist pain with swelling and morning stiffness lasting >1 hour for 3 months. Fatigue and low-grade fever.

physical_exam.joints: Swelling and tenderness of MCP and PIP joints bilaterally, ulnar deviation, rheumatoid nodules on elbows

hidden_data.xray_hands: Periarticular osteopenia, joint space narrowing, marginal erosions at MCP joints

patient_responses.morning: I can't make a fist for at least an hour after waking up
patient_responses.joints: Both hands hurt, especially my knuckles
patient_responses.fatigue: I'm tired all the time, even after resting

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Seropositive RA with erosive changes.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is rheumatoid arthritis. The symmetric polyarthritis + anti-CCP + erosions are diagnostic.
feedback.attending_note: RA management: Methotrexate 15mg weekly + folic acid 1mg daily. Prednisone bridge for rapid symptom control. Monitor CBC, LFTs, creatinine every 3 months. Treat-to-target: remission or low disease activity. Refer to rheumatology.
